overall.headline: The execution kernel is doctrine-grade (deterministic-first, receipts, SSOT, permissions, contracts largely DONE), but the system is nearly blind to its own cost and has almost no revenue organ or learning loop — the single keystone gap is real cost metering (#107 -> #133), which unblocks the entire cost-governance cluster (109/132/134/142/143).

by_cluster.cluster: A. Agent-as-role, registry, invocation discipline, permissions
by_cluster.summary: Strongest structural area: closed callable-worker set (#1/#2), capability guards (#58), per-agent secrets (#57), cost-classed registry (#116); only registry-population and lint items (#117/#123/#135) remain PARTIAL.

by_cluster.cluster: B. Model tiers, cost routing, escalation, local/cloud, vendor failover
by_cluster.summary: Escalation ladder (#3), vendor-neutral routing (#110) and degraded mode (#112) are DONE, but there is only one provider binding so failover (#111) is thin, and scoring/history feedback (#97/#98) plus distillation/shadow-eval (#101/#103) are absent.

by_cluster.cluster: C. Context slicing, budgets, retrieval, caching, versioning, compression
by_cluster.summary: Slicing (#5), micro-batching (#78) and output compression (#89) work, but there is no token/context budget at all (#6 MISSING) and retrieval/caching/versioning (#7/#39/#40) are only half-wired.

by_cluster.cluster: D. Deterministic-first, verification pyramid, receipts, heartbeat, structured output
by_cluster.summary: Deep strength: deterministic-first (#8), code-authored + schema-bearing receipts (#28/#29), structured output (#79); gaps are an unordered pyramid (#27), no real cost receipt (#107), and missing semantic diff (#84).

by_cluster.cluster: E. Job contracts, budgets, bounded change, branch-per-job, single writer, states, gates
by_cluster.summary: Contract discipline is solid: immutable contract (#16), branch-per-job (#15), dependency decomposition (#49), irreversible gate (#60); remaining gaps are max_files/lines caps (#48), typed BLOCKED_EXECUTOR_ENDPOINT (#56) and dry-run-default inversion (#59).

by_cluster.cluster: F. Retry ladder, failure caching, negative KB, success patterns, distillation, audit
by_cluster.summary: Weak learning organ: signatures/patterns exist in SourceA but are unwired from the motor; failure-typed retry ladder (#19), failure-signature suppression (#20) and capability promotion (#137) are all PARTIAL, kill-criteria (#122) and random audit (#105) MISSING.

by_cluster.cluster: G. Event-driven, adaptive polling, ROI preflight, stop conditions, liveness, cost sentinel
by_cluster.summary: The cost/ROI blind spot: only event-driven dispatch (#31) is DONE; daily cost sentinel (#133), spike breaker (#132), waste report (#134) and cost-per-outcome (#143) are all MISSING because spend is never aggregated.

by_cluster.cluster: H. SSOT enforcement, authority, no-2nd-control-plane, compiler, canary, integrator, parallel, Ruflo
by_cluster.summary: Largest cluster and second-strongest: SSOT/authority/no-2nd-plane (#41/#42/#43), prompt + deterministic-workflow compilers (#23/#118), canary (#45/#46) all DONE; PARTIALs are integrator automation, interface contracts, Ruflo stubs and workflow-template breadth; change propagation (#130) MISSING.

by_cluster.cluster: I. Commercial surface / SourceB fulfillment / config / pricing / claims / founder gates / outcome metrics
by_cluster.summary: Biggest and least-built cluster: founder-gate plumbing exists but there is no pricing SSOT (#83), claims registry (#82), customer-config layer (#67-72), fulfillment state machine (#74), revenue receipt (#144) or unlock-score queue (#65); the SourceB fast path (#148) is not even local.

top_gaps.title: Daily cost sentinel
top_gaps.why_high_leverage: unlock 5 and the explicit currency the entire cost-governance cluster (109/132/134/142/143) depends on; without a summed daily spend number none of revenue-linked scaling, spike breaker, waste report or cost-per-outcome can fire. Cost receipts exist but are mostly 0.0 and never aggregated.
top_gaps.next_step: Write a deterministic daily sentinel that scans all cost receipts for the day and emits cost-by-project / by-model / retry / failed-job totals + top-5 workflows.

top_gaps.title: Cost receipt (real token metering)
top_gaps.why_high_leverage: unlock 4; the measurement primitive UNDER #133. Cost fields exist in schema but are hardcoded (0.002/0.01) not measured, so every downstream cost gate (108/133/134/143) is computing on fiction. Instrumenting this is the true keystone that makes the whole G cluster real.
top_gaps.next_step: Instrument intel_low._call_raw to capture real token counts + call count and have executor.build_receipt compute cost from those plus wall-clock runtime and retry count.

top_gaps.title: Pricing SSOT
top_gaps.why_high_leverage: unlock 4; foundational commercial primitive that #130 change-propagation, #82 claims registry and every checkout/landing/FAQ surface must derive from. Price is currently a free-form draft field with no canonical source, so commercial correctness is unguaranteed.
top_gaps.next_step: Create data/pricing_ssot_v1.json (canonical price IDs + display) and make commercial_pulse price_display and any checkout read from it.

top_gaps.title: Dependency unlock score
top_gaps.why_high_leverage: unlock 4; this is the doctrine's own prioritization mechanism (the very metric this report ranks by). Without an unlock_score term in the scorer, the queue cannot rank foundational unblockers (standing identity, canonical queue, cost metering) ahead of cosmetic work — it self-limits every other improvement.
top_gaps.next_step: Add an unlock_score field (0-5) to census metadata and a UNLOCK_WEIGHT term in score_record so foundational unblockers rank first.

top_gaps.title: SourceB commercial fast path
top_gaps.why_high_leverage: unlock 5; the actual revenue pipeline (setup->demo->gate->provision->activate->receipt). It is the highest-unlock point in the audit but currently only a bounded write target — the money path does not exist in any local repo. Everything commercial (67-76, 144) is downstream of standing this up.
top_gaps.next_step: Author the fast path as workflow definition sourceb_voice_provision_v1 in the motor workflow registry, targeting the SourceB repo once it is available locally.

top_gaps.title: Revenue receipt
top_gaps.why_high_leverage: unlock 4; the denominator for cost-per-verified-outcome (#143) and the only proof the commercial organ works. The outbound revenue log exists but has zero real receipts, so revenue is uncounted and unmeasurable.
top_gaps.next_step: Define revenue_receipt_v1 schema (lead_id/payment_id/provisioning_id/trial/captured-call/appointment) and emit one on the first real lead/payment event.

top_gaps.title: Fulfillment state machine
top_gaps.why_high_leverage: unlock 4; the commercial-pulse SM exists for outbound sends but the customer fulfillment states (requested->approved->provisioning->started->done->trial->cancelled) do not. This is the skeleton the entire SourceB fast path (#148) and provisioning gates (#73/#76) hang on.
top_gaps.next_step: Author fulfillment_state_machine_v1 with the doctrine states as a sibling to the commercial-pulse SM, owned by the SourceB fast path.

top_gaps.title: Default-deny cost explosion
top_gaps.why_high_leverage: unlock 4; already structural for the two costliest dimensions (premium escalation, >2 retries via the router ceiling), but the other 6 doctrine dimensions (full-repo context, agents>3, multi-round, browser automation, paid provisioning, long schedule) have no deny-unless-reason guard — the remaining cost-explosion surface.
top_gaps.next_step: Add a single preflight policy check that default-denies the remaining 131 dimensions unless the job contract carries an explicit reason field for that dimension.

top_gaps.title: Retry ladder by failure type
top_gaps.why_high_leverage: unlock 4; the motor is single-tier (fail -> W-INTEL-LOW -> handoff) with no failure-class routing. Wiring the already-computed failure_classes to distinct tiers (syntax->T0, test->T1, architecture->handoff, credential/outage->typed blocker) turns blind retries into cause-based escalation and cuts wasted repair cycles.
top_gaps.next_step: Add a route table in motor/repair keyed on diagnose_deterministic failure_classes dispatching each class to its matching tier.

top_gaps.title: Failure caching (signature)
top_gaps.why_high_leverage: unlock 4; signature computation exists in SourceA but no dispatcher consults it, so the motor re-runs known-blocked jobs and re-burns cost on the same failure. Suppressing re-dispatch on an open blocker signature is direct, compounding waste reduction.
top_gaps.next_step: Add a failure-signature ledger the motor intake checks: on a job whose (repo,error,workflow_step) signature is an open blocker, refuse re-dispatch and refresh only the blocker receipt.

top_gaps.title: Missing-capability -> exact interface
top_gaps.why_high_leverage: unlock 4; handoff packets describe missing capability in prose, not as a machine-readable interface. A typed BLOCKED_EXECUTOR_ENDPOINT (job_class, worker_class, adapter path, binding_env) lets a capability gap be auto-filled by one registry row — turning founder handoffs into self-describing build tickets.
top_gaps.next_step: Add a typed BLOCKED_EXECUTOR_ENDPOINT field to the handoff packet naming the exact missing interface.

top_gaps.title: Revenue-linked scaling
top_gaps.why_high_leverage: unlock 4; the spend cap is policy text with no enforcement — the founder's own system notes 'no enforcement receipt'. Once #133 produces a monthly spend number, one gate in the ROI Judge closes the loop between spend and revenue, the doctrine's core economic safety.
top_gaps.next_step: Build a monthly-spend accumulator from cost receipts and wire a gate that BLOCKS_WITH_REASON new premium dispatch above the applicable cap, emitting an enforcement receipt.

quick_wins.title: Compiler shrinks prompt (entropy metric)
quick_wins.next_step: Emit input_tokens vs output_tokens (byte size) on each compile receipt and add a canary asserting compiled_contract_size < raw_heading_context_size. Compiler already produces the bounded contract; just measure it.

quick_wins.title: Cheap schema repair
quick_wins.next_step: Add a deterministic JSON-repair pass (bracket/quote/trailing-comma fixups) before the single re-prompt in intel_low, fixing most malformed outputs with zero extra model calls.

quick_wins.title: Receipt deduplication (delta only)
quick_wins.next_step: In write_receipt, hash the state-bearing fields and, when identical to the previous receipt for the same job, write a small delta/heartbeat pointer instead of a full duplicate.

quick_wins.title: Output length limits
quick_wins.next_step: Define an output-limits policy (max_summary_words 250, max_risks 5, max_recommendations 3) and enforce it in intel_low._validate by truncating/rejecting oversized arrays. Caps already exist as scattered prompt hints.

quick_wins.title: Founder decision compression
quick_wins.next_step: Define founder_decision_v1 schema (options[], recommended, why[<=3], cost_risk_delta, no_decision_consequence) and have handoff.emit_packet populate it; the decision item already carries kind/detail/receipt/packet.

quick_wins.title: Flaky test registry
quick_wins.next_step: Add data/flaky_test_registry_v1.json (test, flake_rate, known_signature, retry_policy, owner) populated from SourceA _unstable scores; detection already exists, only the persistent registry file is missing.

quick_wins.title: Low-risk auto-promotion
quick_wins.next_step: Add a low_risk_auto_promote predicate that advances typo/docs/fixture/receipt jobs to READY_FOR_MERGE without a decision item; auto-advance-on-success already exists.

quick_wins.title: Avoid agent theater
quick_wins.next_step: Add a CI lint asserting the callable worker set stays <=3 ranks and no job contract requests >1 reasoning agent for risk<high — turns an already-true invariant into a rejectable rule.

strongest_areas: Execution/permission discipline (Cluster A, 7/10 DONE): agent-as-role not process (#1), template!=execution with one min-cost worker per job (#2), capability-based permissions (#58), per-agent secret scoping (#57), cost-classed worker registry (#116).

Deterministic-first + evidence (Cluster D, 8/14 DONE): the doctrine's boundary is structurally enforced — no-LLM deterministic modules (#8), don't-guess-provable-facts recompute (#9), code-authored + schema-bearing receipts (#28/#29), strict structured output (#79), machine-generated human views (#128).

SSOT + authority + compilation (Cluster H, 11/23 DONE): one-owner-per-domain enforcement (#41), authority check before edit (#42), no second control plane (#43), prompt compilation pipeline (#23), deterministic workflow compiler (#118), thin-slice canary with real proof bundles (#45).

Job-contract governance (Cluster E, 7/10 DONE): immutable worker-agnostic contract (#16), branch-per-job never-merge (#15), real dependency-graph decomposition (#49), irreversible-action founder gate (#60), rich partial-success state set (#55).

Cost-safe-by-construction routing: hard router ceiling MAX_AUTOMATIC_RANK=1 as law not config (#3/#131), vendor-neutral binding-by-env-name (#110), first-class degraded-continue mode (#112) — the system fails closed and cheap even though it cannot yet measure its own spend.

full_audit.title: Agent as role not process
evidence: noetfield-sandbox-private/motor/MOTOR_SPEC_v2.md:19-27 (worker classes as closed set with class|mode|role)
noetfield-sandbox-private/motor/adapters/intel_low.py:116-132 (invoke() is function-like: task+payload -> schema-validated proposal, no persistent process)
full_audit.gap: Doctrine's exact per-agent contract fields (Allowed Inputs / Allowed Tools / Required Output Schema) are expressed structurally (capabilities[] + TASK_SCHEMAS + path guard) rather than as one declared agent-role contract object. noos AGENT_REGISTRY is per-repo prose, not machine-enforced.
full_audit.next_step: Add an explicit `allowed_inputs`/`allowed_tools`/`output_schema` triple to each ADAPTER_REGISTRY_v2 worker row and assert it at invoke time so the role contract is data, not just code.
full_audit.cluster: Agent-as-role, registry, invocation discipline, permissions

full_audit.title: Template != execution (registry many, invoke few)
evidence: noetfield-sandbox-private/motor/adapters/router.py:43-71 (dispatch exactly ONE min-cost worker per job; W-HANDOFF structurally unroutable)
noetfield-sandbox-private/motor/MOTOR_SPEC_v2.md:11-17 (pipeline = compiler/planner once -> worker per job -> verifier legs -> repair only on failure)
full_audit.gap: Doctrine default '1 Planner + 1 Worker + 1 Verifier; +1 Repair on fail; +1 Adversarial Reviewer on risk' is implemented except the risk-triggered Adversarial Reviewer — grep finds it only as an unbuilt checklist item (sina-governance-SSOT/docs/1111_UPGRADE_PLANS_v1.md:141 'when AI budget exists'). High-risk currently routes to the founder decision gate instead.
full_audit.next_step: Add a risk-gated W-INTEL-LOW 'adversarial_review' task (read-diff, propose-only) that fires when contract risk=high before the founder gate, receipted like any other leg.
full_audit.cluster: Agent-as-role, registry, invocation discipline, permissions

full_audit.title: Separate thinking / acting / verifying
evidence: noetfield-sandbox-private/motor/MOTOR_SPEC_v2.md:74-79 ('Builder and verifier are separate graph jobs'; 'No model output ever mints truth')
noetfield-sandbox-private/motor/adapters/intel_low.py:11-14 ('Output is ALWAYS a proposal; deterministic validation controls application') = thinking
full_audit.gap: Thinking is quarantined into W-INTEL-LOW-as-proposal and verification into separate workflow identities — fully separated. No gap of substance; only W-VERIFY-EDGE (the sole PASS minter) is spec'd-but-not-yet-live per §7.
full_audit.next_step: Stand up the W-VERIFY-EDGE independent-credential leg so the verifier separation reaches PASS-minting, closing the 'max verdict = CHECK_OK until edge live' caveat.
full_audit.cluster: Agent-as-role, registry, invocation discipline, permissions

full_audit.title: Per-agent secrets (blast radius)
evidence: noetfield-sandbox-private/motor/MOTOR_SPEC_v2.md:88-92 (short-lived per-job repository tokens; secrets in private deployment config)
noetfeld-OS/noetfield-org/ROUTING_MATRIX.md (per-tier 'Secret/Token Access' scoping; T1 'Forbidden: Secret credential handling')
full_audit.gap: Scoping is declared (each worker sees only its own binding_env) but there is no runtime enforcement that a worker process cannot read another worker's env var — isolation is by convention/adapter discipline, not by a sandboxed secret broker.
full_audit.next_step: Inject each worker's binding_env as the ONLY secrets present in its executor subprocess environment (strip all others in scheduler._dispatch_executor) so blast-radius containment is enforced, not trusted.
full_audit.cluster: Agent-as-role, registry, invocation discipline, permissions

full_audit.title: Capability-based permissions
evidence: noetfield-sandbox-private/motor/adapters/det_patch.py:36-46 (_guard fail-closed path allowlist; empty=deny-all = worker write:scoped/branch_only)
noetfield-sandbox-private/motor/adapters/router.py:50 (capability match gate) + intel_low always-proposal = planner write:false
noetfeld-OS/noetfield-org/ROUTING_MATRIX.md (L17 T0-T3 explicit per-tier tool Access + Forbidden lists)
full_audit.gap: The doctrine's literal planner(write:false)/worker(write:scoped)/verifier(write:false,test:true) capability triples are enforced structurally (proposal-only intel, path guard, read-only verifiers) rather than declared as one capability object per role. noos ROUTING_MATRIX is authoritative prose, not a runtime policy engine.
full_audit.next_step: Add a `permissions{write,shell,network,git_commit,deploy}` block to each ADAPTER_REGISTRY_v2 worker row and check it in executor before each side-effect, unifying the currently-scattered guards.
full_audit.cluster: Agent-as-role, registry, invocation discipline, permissions

full_audit.title: Agent registry with cost_class
evidence: noetfield-sandbox-private/motor/registry/ADAPTER_REGISTRY_v2.json (per worker: class_rank 0/1, est_cost_usd_per_job, max_concurrency, reliability, health)
noetfield-sandbox-private/motor/adapters/router.py:68-70 (min-select by class_rank asc, est_cost asc, reliability desc = cost-class routing)
noetfield-sandbox-private/motor/canaries/test_motor_canaries.py:30-37 (RED canary rejects a rank-2 callable row)
full_audit.gap: Motor's registry uses class_rank+est_cost (semantic cost_class) but not the doctrine's literal field names default_model_tier/invoke_when/max_calls. The governance p0-pgr prompt registry that DOES carry model_tier is schema-only — data/p0pgr_prompt_registry_v1.json does not exist on disk (unpopulated).
full_audit.next_step: Create and populate data/p0pgr_prompt_registry_v1.json against its schema (currently absent), giving the governance layer a real cost-classed template registry to match the motor's worker registry.
full_audit.cluster: Agent-as-role, registry, invocation discipline, permissions

full_audit.title: Workflow registry vs dynamic swarm
evidence: noetfield-sandbox-private/motor/MOTOR_SPEC_v2.md:98-100 ('New worker = one registry row'; serialized scheduler, no swarm)
noetfield-sandbox-private/products/sandbox-first-production-law-v1/PARALLEL_SITE_WORKFLOW_MATRIX_v1.md:184 ('runner written ONCE... adding a lane = one map entry, zero new code')
full_audit.gap: The anti-dynamic-swarm principle is fully realized (registry-driven, serialized, no swarm-consensus code anywhere). But a first-class named-workflow catalog is thin: the doctrine's exemplar workflows (repo_repair_v1/storefront_release_v1/pricing_consistency_check_v1/trustfield_partner_audit_v1) are NOT found on disk in any repo, and the p0-pgr registry that would hold problem_class templates is unpopulated (no data file).
full_audit.next_step: Author a data/workflow_registry_v1.json enumerating the named reusable workflows (repo_repair_v1, storefront_release_v1, pricing_consistency_check_v1, trustfield_partner_audit_v1) with their fixed step sequences, referenced by the compiler.
full_audit.cluster: Agent-as-role, registry, invocation discipline, permissions

full_audit.title: Avoid agent theater
evidence: noetfield-sandbox-private/motor/MOTOR_SPEC_v2.md:19-25 (closed 3-callable-role set, no 20-agent council / CEO agent / swarm consensus)
sina-governance-SSOT/SG-Canonical-Library/noetfield-library/P7-DOCTRINE/mechanical-not-prose.md:22 ('prose gates enable theater; mechanical gates prevent it')
full_audit.gap: Anti-theater is embodied by construction (bounded roles, evidence receipts, no swarm) and documented as doctrine, but there is no explicit theater-detection lint — nothing mechanically flags an over-provisioned agent count, oversized context, or council-for-typo pattern. It relies on the architecture never adding those, not on a check that would reject them.
full_audit.next_step: Add a CI lint asserting the callable worker set stays <=3 ranks and that no job contract requests >1 reasoning agent for risk<high, turning 'avoid theater' into a rejectable rule.
full_audit.cluster: Agent-as-role, registry, invocation discipline, permissions

full_audit.title: Short persona prompts
evidence: noetfield-sandbox-private/motor/adapters/intel_low.py:39-59 (PROMPTS are terse task specs: role + strict-JSON schema + input, no long persona)
noetfield-sandbox-private/motor/MOTOR_SPEC_v2.md:4-6 (product/vendor/model/persona names FORBIDDEN in spec, worker classes, prompts)
full_audit.gap: Motor prompts already match the doctrine template (Role / strict output / bounds). The governance packet compiler enforces constraints but there is no explicit max-length/no-persona lint on compiled prompts to prevent future bloat.
full_audit.next_step: Add a compiler assertion capping prompt/persona length and rejecting persona narrative, so the short-prompt discipline survives new templates.
full_audit.cluster: Agent-as-role, registry, invocation discipline, permissions

full_audit.title: Agent retirement
evidence: noetfield-sandbox-private/motor/registry/ADAPTER_REGISTRY_v2.json (health field; rows removable — 'Adding a worker = adding one row')
full_audit.gap: Retirement exists only as a status value (p0-pgr RETIRED) and as founder-gate delegation conditions (noos ledger retires human gates, not agents). No engine detects unused / duplicate / high-failure / superseded templates and proposes retire/merge; the p0-pgr registry that would track template usage stats and failure rates is unpopulated (no data file), so there is nothing to retire from yet.
full_audit.next_step: Add a retirement pass over data/p0pgr_prompt_registry_v1.json (once populated) that flags templates with zero invocations in N days, duplicate problem_class, or failure_rate>threshold and emits a retire/merge proposal receipt.
full_audit.cluster: Agent-as-role, registry, invocation discipline, permissions

full_audit.title: Model escalation not uniformity (tier ladder)
evidence: noetfield-sandbox-private/motor/registry/ADAPTER_REGISTRY_v2.json:3 (rank0 deterministic, rank1 low-cost, rank>=2 does not exist -> HANDOFF)
full_audit.gap: The locked 6-name ladder (T0/T1/T1L/T2/Manual/Deterministic) is collapsed to 3 in every system: motor has rank0/rank1/handoff, p0-pgr has cheap/capable/premium. No distinct T1L (long-context) tier exists anywhere. Motor emits HANDOFF_REQUIRED rather than the doctrine's `status: ESCALATE_REQUIRED` receipt with target_tier/risk_if_not_escalated/files_in_scope/estimated_cost fields.
full_audit.next_step: Add a T1L worker row (class_rank between 1 and handoff, or a distinct binding) to ADAPTER_REGISTRY_v2.json for long-context low-cost work, and standardize the handoff packet to carry the doctrine ESCALATE_REQUIRED field set (target_tier, risk_if_not_escalated, estimated_cost).
full_audit.cluster: Model tiers, cost routing, escalation, local/cloud, vendor failover

full_audit.title: Cheap first-pass classifier
evidence: noetfield-sandbox-private/motor/adapters/intel_low.py:28-46 ('decompose' shrinks a heading into 2-6 jobs; 'classify' task)
full_audit.gap: The cheap decompose emits jobs with title/job_class/instruction/depends_on/risk only. It does NOT produce the doctrine's full shrink field set: project/repo/scope/exclude/required_workflow/founder_gate/model_tier. The cheap pass classifies risk but does not itself assign the downstream model_tier or founder_gate, so it isn't yet the routing-decisive first pass the doctrine describes.
full_audit.next_step: Extend intel_low.py TASK_SCHEMAS['decompose'] jobs_item_required to include scope/exclude/required_workflow/founder_gate/model_tier, and have the compiler use that cheap output to set each job's worker_class/tier before dispatch.
full_audit.cluster: Model tiers, cost routing, escalation, local/cloud, vendor failover

full_audit.title: Local LLM for bulk low-risk
evidence: noetfield-sandbox-private/motor/adapters/intel_low.py:1-14 ('Vendor-free by construction'; openai_json wire style explicitly supports OSS/local servers)
full_audit.gap: The abstraction is designed to allow a local model, but the only concrete binding is a hosted cloud endpoint. No local model (doctrine names Qwen3-14B) is wired, and there is no routing that sends bulk low-risk tasks (log clustering / dup detection / inventory / tagging) specifically to a local model vs cloud. The 'no verifier authority' guarantee IS structurally present (tier1 is advisory-only).
full_audit.next_step: Add a local binding (e.g. MOTOR_INTEL_LOW_URL -> local Ollama/vLLM openai_json endpoint) in deployment.json and route the classify/diagnose bulk job_classes to it, keeping cloud only for the escalated path.
full_audit.cluster: Model tiers, cost routing, escalation, local/cloud, vendor failover

full_audit.title: Cloud only for value-dense steps
evidence: noetfield-sandbox-private/motor/kernel/executor.py:231-234 (value-dense exhaustion routes to handoff, not auto-premium)
full_audit.gap: Enforcement of 'never listing/counting/formatting to cloud' is doctrinal + tier0-first rather than a hard classifier that rejects a cloud dispatch for a trivially-deterministic job_class. A misclassified trivial task could still be packeted to a cloud/premium lane.
full_audit.next_step: Add a lint in the packet compiler / router that blocks capable/premium model_tier when problem_class is a deterministic type (listing/counting/url-check/key-rename) unless a failed cheaper receipt id is attached.
full_audit.cluster: Model tiers, cost routing, escalation, local/cloud, vendor failover

full_audit.title: Model output scoring
full_audit.gap: Raw per-call cost/token/retry data is captured on receipts, but there is NO scoring layer that rolls these into a per-model quality signal (test-pass rate, review-rejection rate, hallucination rate, unnecessary-files count, retry-cost penalty). Nothing computes 'cheap-but-thrice-retried is actually more expensive'. score_plan_queue.py scores WORK items, not model outputs.
full_audit.next_step: Build a deterministic scorer that greps receipts by model, aggregates {est_cost, retries/attempts, summary.failed, changed_files} into a per-model score record, and writes a MODEL_SCORECARD.json.
full_audit.cluster: Model tiers, cost routing, escalation, local/cloud, vendor failover

full_audit.title: Router sees historical performance
evidence: noetfield-sandbox-private/motor/MOTOR_SPEC_v2.md:41 (documented sort: class_rank asc, est_cost asc, reliability desc)
full_audit.gap: The router already CONSUMES a `reliability` field as a tiebreaker, so the hook exists — but the value is a static constant 1.0, never recomputed from receipts. There is no feedback loop reading historical receipt outcomes back into reliability/est_cost. It is 'router could see history' not 'router sees history'. Doctrine's 'internal benchmark not vendor advertising' is not built.
full_audit.next_step: Feed the point-97 MODEL_SCORECARD back into ADAPTER_REGISTRY reliability/est_cost_usd_per_job via a scheduled deterministic updater, so route() ranks on measured history.
full_audit.cluster: Model tiers, cost routing, escalation, local/cloud, vendor failover

full_audit.title: Escalation by failure-type not feeling
evidence: noetfield-sandbox-private/products/sandbox-first-production-law-v1/COST_INTELLIGENT_EXECUTION_POLICY_v1.md:101 (escalation_reason MUST be a real FAILED cheaper receipt id or 'risk_high')
noetfield-sandbox-private/motor/kernel/executor.py:204-234 (failure -> repair pipeline on failed_checks -> handoff on exhaustion; no feeling-based jump)
full_audit.gap: The motor cannot escalate at all automatically (structural ceiling), so the failure-typed escalation contract lives in the sandbox policy + handoff packets rather than an executing escalator. The doctrine's specific '2 bounded T1 attempts failed' counter is enforced only as repair_cycles budget, not as an explicit attempt-type ladder.

full_audit.title: Context slicing
full_audit.gap: Slicing is structural (each Job Contract carries only its own instruction+context dict; the sole LLM adapter intel_low.invoke sends `instruction + json.dumps(context)`, never whole repo/conversation; governance packets say 'Workers receive ONLY this packet — never P0 content'). But the doctrine's explicit five-part slice (relevant files + SSOT excerpt + current diff + last relevant receipt) is NOT assembled into the context — context is a free-form dict of spans/probes/skeleton refs, with no diff or SSOT-excerpt retriever populating it.
full_audit.next_step: Add a deterministic context assembler in intake that populates contract.context with {relevant_files[], ssot_excerpt, current_diff, last_receipt_ref} from the target repo before the intel_low call, instead of only span/probe metadata.
full_audit.cluster: Context slicing, budgets, retrieval, caching, versioning, compression

full_audit.title: Per-agent context budget
full_audit.gap: Every 'budget' on disk is a COST/repair-cycle budget (contract.budget = {max_cost_usd, max_repair_cycles}; noos model-router budget = max_usd_per_run/day), never a token/context budget per role. There are no per-role token ceilings (Classifier 4-8K, Planner 12-24K, Worker 20-60K, Reviewer diff+criteria), no token counting, and no 'over budget -> summarize/retrieve narrowly' path. The doctrine's context-budget concept is entirely absent from code.
full_audit.next_step: Add a token-budget table keyed by worker_class/job_class to the job contract (e.g. classifier 8000, worker 60000) and enforce it in intel_low._call_raw by measuring prompt tokens and refusing/narrowing when exceeded.
full_audit.cluster: Context slicing, budgets, retrieval, caching, versioning, compression

full_audit.title: Retrieval before reading
full_audit.gap: The file-tree->targeted-blob half exists: census/emit_headings pull `git/trees/...?recursive=1` then read only specific blobs (package.json/pyproject/Makefile) rather than whole repos, and the noos router's T0 tier is deterministic grep/check/validate at zero LLM spend. But there is no dependency-map step and no grep/ripgrep-then-targeted-line-range retrieval feeding an agent's context — the intel_low adapter receives no retrieved code at all, so the full 'file tree -> grep -> dep map -> targeted files -> line ranges' ladder is not implemented end-to-end.
full_audit.next_step: Insert a ripgrep-backed retrieval step (grep symbol -> collect hit files -> extract enclosing line ranges) that runs before any intel_low skeleton/repair call and injects only the matched ranges into contract.context.
full_audit.cluster: Context slicing, budgets, retrieval, caching, versioning, compression

full_audit.title: Cache stable contexts as registry refs
full_audit.gap: Several stable contexts ARE externalized to versioned registries loaded by reference: ADAPTER_REGISTRY_v2.json (allowed tools/model policy, loaded by router.load_registry), deployment.json (repo ownership + allowed_paths/deploy topology, loaded by intake), noos model-router.yml (model policy, schema+version pinned). But the set is incomplete vs the doctrine list — no pricing SSOT, branch policy, or risk matrix registry — and registry entries carry version/schema fields but not content HASH references, so 'version/hash reference' is only half-met.
full_audit.next_step: Add a content-hash field to each registry (adapter/deployment/model-router) and record the hash in receipts so a cached context can be proven unchanged; add the missing pricing/branch-policy/risk-matrix registry files.
full_audit.cluster: Context slicing, budgets, retrieval, caching, versioning, compression

full_audit.title: Context versioning (policy refs)
full_audit.gap: Reference-by-name-and-version is the house style: packet schema references RUNTIME_CONTINUITY_LAW_v1 and spec_path by name, model-router pins schema/version, governance docs cite POLICY_v1 names rather than inlining. But there is no runtime resolver that takes a 'Apply MODEL_ESCALATION_POLICY_v1' reference and retrieves just the relevant section on demand — the pattern is documentary/convention, not an executable retrieve-by-ref mechanism.
full_audit.next_step: Build a small policy-ref resolver (name+version -> canonical file section) that packets/adapters call to fetch only the cited clause at execution time, so policy text is never inlined into context.
full_audit.cluster: Context slicing, budgets, retrieval, caching, versioning, compression

full_audit.title: Batch similar work
full_audit.gap: Homogeneous work IS batched deterministically: emit_batch/build_records process the plan queue in one pass, census classifies all candidate docs in a single traversal, and the language_gate has a batch markdown generator. However these are deterministic (no-LLM) scans; there is no batched LLM classify/summarize call — the intel_low adapter is invoked one item at a time, so the doctrine's 'classify/summarize once' for model calls is not realized.
full_audit.next_step: Add a batched intel_low task that accepts an array of homogeneous items (e.g. classify N queue items) in one schema-constrained call, guarded by a context-size check so the batch never bloats the prompt.
full_audit.cluster: Context slicing, budgets, retrieval, caching, versioning, compression

full_audit.title: Micro-batching 5-20
full_audit.gap: The 20-item micro-batch cap is implemented exactly to spec: MAX_ISSUES=20 caps emitted headings to min(20, eligible) homogeneous queue items, and intel_low decomposition is capped at 6 jobs (jobs[:6]). This matches the 5-20 homogeneous-items rule and avoids the '1000 unrelated docs' failure mode. Minor gap: no explicit lower-bound (5) batching-efficiency check, and the cap is a constant rather than adaptive to context size.
full_audit.next_step: Optional: make the 20-cap adaptive — shrink the batch when accumulated item size approaches the (to-be-added) context budget rather than using a fixed constant.
full_audit.cluster: Context slicing, budgets, retrieval, caching, versioning, compression

full_audit.title: Tool output compression
full_audit.gap: Output compression is real and multi-layered: SourceA's execution_spine keeps only stdout[-12000:]/stderr[-8000:] plus exit_code and a one-line error_signature; motor det_exec keeps `exit {code}` + last 200 chars of combined output; receipts store summary counts {total, passed, failed} instead of full logs. This delivers first/last-lines + exit code + summary counts. Gap vs doctrine wording: the tail is a fixed CHAR budget, not 'last 100 relevant LINES', and there is no relevance filter selecting error-adjacent lines.
full_audit.next_step: Replace the fixed char-tail with a line-based compressor that emits first error line + last 100 lines + exit code + counts, and drops known-noise lines.
full_audit.cluster: Context slicing, budgets, retrieval, caching, versioning, compression

full_audit.title: Error signature extraction
full_audit.gap: A basic error signature exists: execution_spine._error_signature(stderr, exit_code) returns the last stderr line truncated to 240 chars (or exit_N), stored as ExecutionRecord.error_signature and surfaced to the repair loop; failure_classifier buckets failures into a fixed FAILURE_CLASSES enum. But none of the doctrine's normalization is done — no path normalization, no timestamp stripping, no stack-trace dedup, no grouping of like errors before the model. Two runs with different tmp paths/timestamps produce different signatures.
full_audit.next_step: Extend _error_signature to normalize (strip abs paths to repo-relative, remove ISO timestamps/hex ids, collapse repeated stack frames) and hash the normalized form so identical errors group to one signature.
full_audit.cluster: Context slicing, budgets, retrieval, caching, versioning, compression

full_audit.title: Prompt prefix caching
full_audit.gap: The prompt is STRUCTURED for prefix caching: intel_low builds `PROMPTS[task] + payload` where PROMPTS holds the stable role+schema prefix and only the job-specific payload varies (packets likewise separate stable governance from context_summary). But no provider prompt-caching is actually engaged — the openai_json/chat_json bodies set no cache_control breakpoint, and the free/cheap endpoints targeted may not support it. The cacheable shape exists; the cache is never claimed. (No cache_control usage found anywhere in the code repos.)
full_audit.next_step: When MOTOR_INTEL_LOW_STYLE targets a caching-capable endpoint, mark the static PROMPTS[task] prefix with the provider's cache_control breakpoint so only the payload is uncached.
full_audit.cluster: Context slicing, budgets, retrieval, caching, versioning, compression

full_audit.title: Output length limits
full_audit.gap: Length caps exist but not the doctrine's exact structured limits: packet context_summary is maxLength 1200, intel_low classify rationale is instructed '<=200 chars', decomposition is capped at 6 jobs. Missing are the named limits max_summary_words:250 / max_risks:5 / max_recommendations:3 as an enforced output schema — nothing in code caps a risks[] or recommendations[] array length, and the word/char caps are prompt hints (classify) or field maxLength, not a shared reusable limit policy.
full_audit.next_step: Define an output-limits policy (max_summary_words 250, max_risks 5, max_recommendations 3) and enforce it in intel_low._validate by truncating/rejecting proposals whose arrays or summary exceed the caps.
full_audit.cluster: Context slicing, budgets, retrieval, caching, versioning, compression

full_audit.title: Evidence links not restating
